text: Philosophy of science examines how science works. Kuhn (1962) challenged the view of science as steady accumulation: instead, 'paradigm shifts' disrupt 'normal science.' Popper: falsifiability demarcates science from non-science — a theory must be testable and refutable. Scientific realism vs. instrumentalism: do theories describe reality or just p
Reproducibility crisis (2010s): many published findings fail to replicate — methodology reform ongoing.
